issue_title: OOME during GCP upload resulted in creation of empty blob
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`): 7.9.1

**Plugins installed**: Cloud

**JVM version** (`java -version`): Bundled

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system): Cloud

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:

The master node reported an OOME while writing the `RepositoryData` blob at `2021-03-17T04:30:56.129`. However it apparently created an empty blob rather than failing outright:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5058284/115565849-a40a9480-a2b1-11eb-9abf-a5d4b1560c03.png)

This rendered the repository unusable: an empty blob is not valid at this location.

commit_message:
Ensure GCS Repository Metadata Blob Writes are Atomic (#72051)

In the corner case of uploading a large (>5MB) metadata blob we did not set content validation
requirement on the upload request (we automatically have it for smaller requests that are not resumable
uploads). This change sets the relevant request option to enforce a MD5 hash check when writing
`BytesReference` to GCS (as is the case with all but data blob writes)

closes #72018

commit_message:
Update joda dependency to latest 2.10.10  backport(#72052) (#72490)

Latest JDKs are shipping with timezone data 2021a which is also included
in latest joda. In order to have the timezone information consistent in
both joda and java.time joda dependnecy has to be updated

closes #72028

commit_message:
Revert "Fix ListenableFuture Resolving Listeners under Mutex" (#72055)

We think that this PR, along with #70373, has exposed some legitimate bugs
that we need to address. They're currently causing test failures that are hard
to scope and mute. For now we'll revert this commit, with the plan to
reintroduce it along with the bug fixes.

Relates to #72033, #72031.

issue_title: Using IP in date histogram aggregate causes array_index_out_of_bounds_exception
commit_message:
Docvalueformat errors (#73121)

Improve the error message when inconsistent mappings cause doc value formatting errors.  For example, trying to format a binary encoded IP address as a UTF8 string often fails with something unexpected, like `ArrayIndexOutOfBounds`.  This change catches that and wraps it with a message suggesting the user check their mappings.  Also gets rid of anonymous instances for doc value formatters, which made it hard to see what format was failing to be applied.

commit_message:
[ML] Avoid direct action on system index in evaluate REST tests (#72069)

Refreshing all indices seems to potentially touch on the `.ml-config`
index which is a system index. That can cause a depracation warning
for directly accessing system indices. This commit changes the
refresh request to just be on the data index the test uses.

Closes #72046

Adjust AutoFollowIT#testDataStreams_autoFollowAfterDataStreamCreated() test (#74468)

Adjust ccr data stream test that tests following a backing index after the
data stream has been auto followed.

Avoid that a data stream matches with a builtin template that uses ilm.
The manual rollover that happens in this test, may cause ilm to add
`index.lifecycle.indexing_complete` setting, which causes explicit
follow index api call to fail in this test

Closes #72062

issue_body:
Seen in a build failure:

```
  1> java.lang.NullPointerException: null

  1> 	at org.elasticsearch.xpack.core.indexing.AsyncTwoPhaseIndexer.onBulkResponse(AsyncTwoPhaseIndexer.java:546) [x-pack-core-8.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:8.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
  1> 	at org.elasticsearch.xpack.core.indexing.AsyncTwoPhaseIndexer.lambda$onSearchResponse$9(AsyncTwoPhaseIndexer.java:511) [x-pack-core-8.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:8.0.0-SNAPSHOT]

  1> 	at org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.ThreadContext$ContextPreservingRunnable.run(ThreadContext.java:669) [elasticsearch-8.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar:8.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
  1> 	at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1128) [?:?]
  1> 	at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:628) [?:?]
  1> 	at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:834) [?:?]
```

The corresponding line in code:

https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/d1c3b71384766da48ec26a7a3cc61e373e7fdd39/x-pack/plugin/core/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/core/indexing/AsyncTwoPhaseIndexer.java#L579

It looks like we need another check for `null` here.

Note: This can only happen if a machine runs super slow. The minimal throttling wait time is `10ms`, so in order to run into this issue, it must have spent `10ms` in the surrounding code.
commit_message:
[Transform] improve throttling robustness (#72170)

A scheduled runnable can get reset to null while it is supposed to get
rescheduled, this change adds a local variable, that way a background
change does not result in a NPE.
Note: the runable uses RunOnce(secured by atomics) to ensure single execution

fixes #72066

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
Composite aggs should work with sorted indices
**Steps to reproduce**:

commit_message:
Fix composite early termination on sorted (#72101)

I broke composite early termination when reworking how aggregations'
contact for `getLeafCollector` around early termination in #70320. We
didn't see it in our tests because we weren't properly emulating the
aggregation collection stage. This fixes early termination by adhering
to the new contract and adds more tests.

Closes #72078

Co-authored-by: Benjamin Trent <4357155+benwtrent@users.noreply.github.com>

commit_message:
Match IPv6 addresses without compressed id from settings (#77404)

Make sure we normalize IPv6 addresses from the settings to the compressed form before we
match them with node ips. Node IPv6 addresses are all represented with the scope id
in the compressed form.

Closes #72091

commit_message:
[Test] more liberal SSL error message matching (#72369)

The error message matching is no longer JVM vendor specific. This is
better because the vendors often take turns to upgrade/backport which
means error messages are more for eventual consistency.

commit_message:
Release store after MultiChunkTransfer in recovery (#72153)

Today a recovery holds a reference to the underlying directory, via the
store, and the `MultiChunkTransfer` holds open one of the files in the
directory. However on cleanup we may close the store before the
`MultiChunkTransfer`, which is invalid since we should have closed all
files first.

This commit records the `MultiChunkTransfer` as an independent referer
to the store ensuring that the files are closed first.

Closes #71919
Closes #72144
Relates #72037

issue_title: Combined Fields query `minimum_should_match` discrepancy for integer values
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** 7.13.0-SNAPSHOT
**Plugins installed**: n/a
**JVM version**: bundled
**OS version**: Windows 10

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:

When testing the new [Combined Fields query](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/71213), I noticed a discrepancy with the minimum_should_match field which may be a bug. When sending an integer value for the `minimum_should_match` property it returns:

```
"type" : "x_content_parse_exception",
"reason" : "[1:115] [combined_fields] minimum_should_match doesn't support values of type: VALUE_NUMBER",
```

While it's described as a string [in the documentation](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch///reference/master/query-dsl-combined-fields-query.html#combined-field-top-level-params), examples elsewhere in the [docs, show](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch///reference/master/query-dsl-query-string-query.html#query-string-min-should-match-multi) that an integer can be used. For the Match query, we have integration tests successfully sending an integer value too.

**Steps to reproduce**:

Send the following search:

```
POST http://127.0.0.1:9200/project/_search
{
    "query": {
        "combined_fields": {
            "query": "hello world",
            "fields": [
                "description",
                "myOtherField"
            ],
            "minimum_should_match": 2,
            "auto_generate_synonyms_phrase_query": false,
            "operator": "or",
            "zero_terms_query": "all",
            "boost": 1.1,
            "_name": "combined_fields"
        }
    }
}
```

**Expected Result**:

The search executes successfully.

**Actual Result**:

```
"type" : "x_content_parse_exception",
"reason" : "[1:115] [combined_fields] minimum_should_match doesn't support values of type: VALUE_NUMBER",
```

Sending the same query with the value as a string succeeds.

**NOTE**

Sending and integer value for this property in a Match query succeeds, so I'd expect the same behaviour in Combined Fields.

```
{
    "query": {
        "match": {
            "description": {
                "analyzer": "standard",
                "auto_generate_synonyms_phrase_query": false,
                "fuzziness": "AUTO:3,6",
                "fuzzy_rewrite": "top_terms_blended_freqs_10",
                "fuzzy_transpositions": true,
                "lenient": true,
                "minimum_should_match": 2,
                "operator": "or",
                "query": "hello world",
                "boost": 1.1,
                "_name": "named_query"
            }
        }
    }
}
```
commit_message:
Fix `minimum_should_match` for `combined_fields` query (#72152)

The `minimum_should_match` parameter for queries like `match` or `query_string"
accepts either string values like "2" or "25%", numeric values and in most older
cases also `null` as a no-op parameter. We also use examples for these different
values in our documentation, so we should also support these value types for the
new `combined_fields` query. This changes the ConstructingObjectParser used in
CombinedFieldsQueryBuilder so it also accepts integer value types and adds a
parsing test for the different cases.

Closes #72148

issue_title: SQL: request timeout is not honoured
issue_body:
All requests, [initial](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/4dc978e840dc89df0569468958ae455de7f32557/x-pack/plugin/sql/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/sql/execution/search/Querier.java#L115) or subsequent paginations, use the `page_timeout`, whose value's either taken from the config/request or default. 
Expected is to have the `request_timeout` config's value used for the initial request timeout.
commit_message:
SQL: fix use of requestTimeout and pageTimeout query parameters (#79360)

Resolves #72151 The _sql endpoint offers a `page_timeout` parameter for
customizing how long scroll contexts should be kept open (if needed) and
a `request_timeout` parameter which the docs describe as "Timeout before
the request fails.". Currently, the value of the `page_timeout`
parameter is used as the `timeout` in subsequent _search requests and
not as the timeout in the `scroll` configuration. For the `scroll`
configuration, SQL uses the `request_timeout` parameter. This PR
addresses the issue by swapping the uses of `page_timeout` and
`request_timeout` in querier. Additionally, the PR removes some unused
artifacts that might have caused some confusion: - The `timeout` and
`keepAlive` fields in `Querier`. Instead, `Querier` directly uses the
according fields in `SqlConfiguration`. - The `SqlConfiguration`
parameter from `ScrollCursor.clear`, it's not used but required an
instance of `SqlConfiguration` with all default values. - One overloaded
constructor of `SqlConfiguration` that was only used for calling
`ScrollCursor.clear` (and some tests) and used default values for an
(arbitrary?) subset of the fields. - The fields related to async
requests in `SqlConfiguration`. I'm a bit unsure about this one but the
fields are never read and it does not seem like an SQL specific concern.
The whole creation of the async tasks is handled in
`TransportSqlQueryAction` and the downstream components do not require
the information.

commit_message:
[ML] moving inference pipeline tests to allow consistent cleanup (#72686)

Yaml tests are flaky as the stats index could be just recently created when we try to cleanup after the test run.

This fixes that by moving the tests into a ESRestTestCase class and waiting for the .ml-stats indices to be created before running the post test cleanup.

closes: #72189

issue_title: IP filter accepts invalid setting but then fails during cluster state application
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`): observed in 7.11.2 & 7.12.0, but likely others too.

**Plugins installed**: Cloud

**JVM version** (`java -version`): Bundled

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system): Cloud

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:

It is possible to set an invalid value for `xpack.security.transport.filter.allow` via the Put Cluster Settings API, and the invalid value is persisted in the cluster state, but this raises an exception during cluster state application which causes the cluster to enter a loop in which the master node repeatedly fails, wins another election, attempts to apply the invalid value, and fails again. The cluster is effectively unavailable while in this state. If the invalid value is set as a persistent setting then this cannot even be resolved via a full cluster restart.

You can work around this by stopping all the master-eligible nodes, running `elasticsearch-node remove-settings xpack.security.transport.filter.allow` on each of them, and then starting them up again. Alternatively you can disable IP filtering by setting `xpack.security.transport.filter.enabled: false` and `xpack.security.http.filter.enabled: false` in `elasticsearch.yml`, which will allow the cluster to form, and then the persistent setting can be fixed.

Not really sure which area covers this, is it security or distrib? I've guessed distrib.

**Steps to reproduce**:

Execute this on an otherwise healthy cluster:

```
PUT /_cluster/settings
{
  "persistent": {
    "xpack.security.transport.filter.allow": "https://example.com/"

}

commit_message:
Adjust SearchableSnapshotsLicenseIntegTests.testShardAllocationOnInvalidLicense (#77757)

This tests sometimes fails because it expects the last PostStartTrialRequest
to always "upgrade" the current license that it just nullified to a trial
license; but there is a race in this test with the LicenceService that detects
that no license exists in the cluster state (because the test set it to null)
and self generates a trial license for the cluster too. When the self generation
is processed before the PostStartTrialRequest the latter will return a
TRIAL_ALREADY_ACTIVATED response.

Since the purpose of this test is to verify that the searchable snapshot shards
failed when the license change and came back when the trial license if activated
again, I think we can just adjust the test to accommodate for the 2 types of
responses.

Closes #72329

issue_body:
We added the ability to allow mounting an index from an ILM policy in the `hot` phase after rollover, however, these indices are automatically sent to the cold nodes. We need to change ILM such that we pass in `_tier_preference: data_hot` when they are mounted in the hot phase.

We'll also want to ensure they are migrated between phases appropriately.

commit_message:
Fix S3HttpHandler chunked-encoding handling (#72378)

The `S3HttpHandler` reads the contents of the uploaded blob, but if the
upload used chunked encoding then the reader would skip one or more
`\r\n` sequences if they appeared at the start of a chunk.

This commit reworks the reader to be stricter about its interpretation
of chunks, and removes some indirection via streams since we can work
pretty much entirely on the underlying `BytesReference` instead.

Closes #72358

commit_message:
Enable wait/blocked time accounting (#77935)

Enable HotThreads wait/blocked time accounting

HotThreads has wait and blocked time accounting functionality, however
the reported timings for the "wait" and "block" reports are always 0,
because thread contention monitoring isn't enabled in the JVM. This
change fixes that by enabling/disabling thread contention monitoring
while we are running the timing capture logic.

Closes #72376

commit_message:
Increase implicit suite timeout to 40 mins (#72495)

The MixedClusterClientYamlTestSuiteIT test suite has been ordained with a 40 mins timeout.
But the ElasticsearchTestBasePlugin overrides the suite annotation,
using a system prop, to 30 mins; see also SysGlobals#SYSPROP_TIMEOUT_SUITE .
This PR increases the value for the system prop override to 40 mins,
to match the above suites declarations.

Closes #72393

commit_message:
[DOCS] Use kibana_system when setting up Kibana security (#72433)

The `kibana_system` user is specifically intended for use within
Kibana, and has access to all the privileges that are needed or useful
to Kibana. We should advise admins to use that user (rather than
`elastic`) when they set up Kibana for the first time.

Recommending `elastic` puts this at odds with the Kibana setup docs,
and increases the security risk for customers, yet provides no
substantial benefit (other than having a single password to deal with).

commit_message:
Fix race condition in XPackUsageRestCancellationIT (#80079)

Ensure that the blocking XPackUsageAction is executed before cancelling
the request to ensure that the action is not cancelled at the beginning
of the TransportMasterAction.

Closes #72471

[ML] Fixes empty overall_buckets response (#72542)

When the response was empty we were incorrectly returning an
empty array called "jobs" rather than "overall_buckets".
This PR corrects this edge case.

Fixes #72478

commit_message:
Validate forced awareness allocation setting (#72810)

Today any setting under `cluster.routing.allocation.awareness.force.**`
is accepted, but only those matching `...force.*.values` are actually
valid. Accepting other things means that we cannot apply the resulting
cluster state, which is disastrous. This commit adds settings validation
to enforce the expected structure of these settings.

Closes #72524

issue_title: [Transform] transform fails during rolling upgrade with Failed to load transform configuration
issue_body:
**Affected versions:** 7.12
**Fixed in:** 7.12.2

Transforms don't need to be stopped during a rolling upgrade, but can continue to run. A transform that runs on a to upgrade node will be automatically relocated to another node.

This relocation can fail with _Failed to load transform configuration_ if at least 2 transforms run at the time of the upgrade on the same node.

**Mitigation:**

Check the status of your transforms after the upgrade using the UI or API. In case the transform failed with the above error, `force` stop and start the transform again using the UI or API:

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/stop-transform.html
https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/start-transform.html

As an alternative you can suspend transforms during the rolling upgrade, however this is not necessary, there are no downsides in restarting them as explained.

**Fix:**

Transform node allocation needs to check that shards are allocated.

Fix FollowingEngineTests#testOptimizeMultipleVersions (#75583)

In certain concurrent indexing scenarios where there are deletes
executed and then a new indexing operation, the following engine
considers those as updates breaking one of the assumed invariants.

Closes #72527

issue_title: Instances with small heaps and wildcard searches may run OOM
issue_body:
A new strategy to manage the GeoIP database has been recently introduced that stores the database as binary data in an index. If a wild card search includes this new index (.geoip_databases) it can cause unnecessary memory pressure and for small heaps may cause the JVM to run OOM (Out Of Memory). 

#72377 was introduced to help prevent the wildcard search from hitting this new index. However, after that issue was merged it was realized that indices with both `system` and `hidden` flags is not currently fully supported. 

Multiple solutions have been discussed, but the likely fix here is to restrict wildcard expansion to only include new system indexes such as the .geoip_databases and revert #72377.
commit_message:
Protect newly introduced system indices fully (#74186)

This change updates the way we handle net new system indices, which are
those that have been newly introduced and do not require any BWC
guarantees around non-system access. These indices will not be included
in wildcard expansions for user searches and operations. Direct access
to these indices will also not be allowed for user searches.

The first index of this type is the GeoIp index, which this change sets
the new flag on.

Closes #72572

commit_message:
Clean up Node Shutdown metadata in test cleanup (#72726)

tests, as it causes unrelated tests to fail if a test leaves node
shutdown metadata in place.

commit_message:
[Transform] Fix rolling upgrade regression introduced in #72533 (#72666)

#72533 introduced a regression, causing transforms to timeout/fail.
With this change transform only waits for 1 active shard(primary) as waiting for all can block during
rolling upgrade

fixes #72617
relates #72533

commit_message:
[TEST] CCSDuelIT to not check successful shards (#75062)

CCSDuelIT sometimes fails due to an unexpected number of shards returned in the refresh response. We create an index with a certain number of shards and no replicas, and sometimes the number of shards refresh is higher by one than the shards we created. This is not symptom of a problem though, as we are checking that there are no failures, but rather indicates a relocation happening, during which both copies of the same shard have been refreshed.

Closes #61258
Closes #72637
Closes #67754

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system): `Linux hostname 3.10.0-1127.18.2.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Sun Jul 26 15:27:06 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux`

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
Expected index sorting to speed up AND conjunctions and not to raise illegal_state_exception.

**Steps to reproduce**:

Used index structure:
```json
{
  "index_name": {
    "mappings": {
      "dynamic": "false",
      "properties": {
        "brand_id": { "type": "keyword" },
        "category_id": { "type": "keyword" },
        "status_id": { "type": "keyword" },
        "user_id": { "type": "keyword" },

      }
    },
    "settings": {
      "index": {
        "number_of_shards": 3,
        "refresh_interval": "1h",
        "sort": {
          "field": ["category_id", "brand_id", "status_id", "created_at"],
          "order": ["asc", "asc", "asc", "desc"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Search query:
```json
{
  "_source": false,
  "size": 24,
  "query": {
    "bool": {
      "filter": [
        {
          "term": {
            "category_id": 197
          }
        },
        {
          "term": {
            "brand_id": 51
          }
        },
        {
          "term": {
            "status_id": 2
          }
        },
        {
          "range": {
            "price": {
              "gte": 2.0,
              "lte": 5.0
            }
          }
        }
      ],
      "must_not": {
        "terms": {
          "user_id": [12345]
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sort": {
    "created_at": "desc"
  }
}
```

And then the raised exception:
```json
{
  "error": {
    "root_cause": [
      {
        "type": "illegal_state_exception",
        "reason": "topDocs already consumed"
      }
    ],
    "type": "search_phase_execution_exception",
    "reason": "all shards failed",
    "phase": "query",
    "grouped": true,

      }
    }
  },
  "status": 500
}
```

- Index contains about 100M documents and weighs about 10GiB
- Peaks to 12.000 search requests per minute

This was an experiment to improve search query performance and I assumed that [index sorting](https://www.elastic.co/blog/index-sorting-elasticsearch-6-0) would speed up AND conjuctions given that my query is fairly simple. However, I've had mixed results. Some queries ran faster and others started raising illegal_state_exception exceptions. I've tried changing number_of_shards, sorting criteria but the problem persisted. It got worse when I tried putting everything into 1 shard and would raise an exception on every 2nd request.
commit_message:
Fix early termination of search request with sort optimization (#72683)

The query phase applies an optimization when sorting by a numeric field.
This optimization doesn't handle early termination correctly when `timeout`
and/or `terminate_after` are used. An IAE exception is thrown at the shard
level when the timeout is reached.
This commit fixes the bug, early terminated exceptions are correctly caught
and the result is computed from the documents that the shard was able to collect
before the termination.

Closes #72661

issue_title: Stack logging in Docker prevents container recreation
issue_body:
When deploying Elasticsearch in Docker with `ES_LOG_STYLE=file` to use stack logging (#65778), the container may end with the `CrashLoopBackOff` status if the container is ~restarted~ recreated.

### Step to reproduction with ECK on k8s:

- Deploy Elasticsearch using this manifest:

```yaml
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: ananas
spec:
  version: 7.12.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 1
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          env:
          - name: ES_LOG_STYLE
            value: file
```

- Wait for ES to be deployed:

```sh
> k get es,pods
NAME                                                HEALTH   NODES   VERSION   PHASE   AGE
elasticsearch.elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/ananas   green    1       7.12.0    Ready   39s

NAME                      READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
pod/ananas-es-default-0   1/1     Running   0          42s
```

- Force a container restart:

```sh
> k exec ananas-es-default-0 -- kill -15 1
```

The container status then oscillates between `Error` and `CrashLoopBackOff`

```sh
> > k get es,pods
NAME                                                HEALTH    NODES   VERSION   PHASE             AGE
elasticsearch.elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/ananas   unknown           7.12.0    ApplyingChanges   4m49s

NAME                      READY   STATUS             RESTARTS   AGE
pod/ananas-es-default-0   0/1     CrashLoopBackOff   4          4m46s
```                                                                                                                                                                                                            

### What's going on?

The ES log reveals that `docker-entrypoint.sh` fails because `log4j2.file.properties` has already been moved.

```sh
> k logs ananas-es-default-0               
mv: cannot stat '/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/log4j2.file.properties': No such file or directory
```

Here is the culprit:

https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/cbb2ca28261675be7c231c0e4e1d4f41dfeb3d52/distribution/docker/src/docker/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh#L67

Could we just replace the `mv` by a `cp -f`?
commit_message:
Allow container restarts with file logging (#73101)

Closes #72702. It wasn't possible to restart an Elasticsearch Docker
container when using `ES_LOG_STYLE=file`, and now it is.

issue_title: Frozen shards remain UNASSIGNED if there is no frozen tier at the time of creation
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`):
7.13-Snapshot (on the cloud)

**Plugins installed**: []

**JVM version** (`java -version`):

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system):
(Cloud)

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
This is a bit involved. And there appears to be 2 parts to this issue. I found these related issues when testing enabling a frozen tier through autoscaling (0 -> 1) in the cloud. 

Here is what I did:

**ISSUE 1**
1. Create a index
```
PUT test/_doc/1
{ "test" : "test" }
```
2. Create a snapshot based on that index
```
PUT /_snapshot/found-snapshots/snapshot_1?wait_for_completion=true
{
   "indices" : "test"
}
```
3. Mount that snapshot 
```
POST /_snapshot/found-snapshots/snapshot_1/_mount?wait_for_completion=true&storage=shared_cache
{
  "index": "test", 
  "renamed_index": "frozen_test1"
}
```

The first issue immediately presents itself - the new index `frozen_test1` will be UNASSIGNED and the entire cluster will be marked unhealthy `RED`. This is a serious issue because no cloud plans can be applied to an unhealthy cluster. More specifically, this means an autoscaling plan cannot be applied to enable the frozen tier so that the new index could be assigned. 

**ISSUE 2**
4. Continuing from the above, enable a frozen tier manually in the cloud UI
5. I would hope at this point the `frozen_test1` would be assigned since a frozen tier now exists.
6. Unfortunately, that is the not case; the index remains unassigned. 
```
GET _cluster/allocation/explain

{
  "index" : "frozen_test1",
  "shard" : 0,
  "primary" : true,
  "current_state" : "unassigned",
  "unassigned_info" : {
    "reason" : "NEW_INDEX_RESTORED",
    "at" : "2021-05-05T17:55:43.387Z",
    "details" : "restore_source[found-snapshots/snapshot_1]",
    "last_allocation_status" : "no"
  },

        "server_name" : "instance-0000000001.5fc50a1e728f4941bac1f662d41f5fee",
        "availability_zone" : "ece-zone-0",
        "xpack.installed" : "true",
        "data" : "frozen",
        "instance_configuration" : "data.frozen",
        "transform.node" : "false",

      },

        {

commit_message:
Autoscale frozen tier into existence (#73435)

This commit adds two related changes:
* ILM WaitForDataTierStep
* Autoscaling frozen_existence decider

The first part ensures that we wait mounting an index until a node that
can hold the index is available, avoiding a failed restore and red
cluster state. This is in particular important for the frozen phase, but
is done generically in the searchable snapshot action.

The second part triggers on indices in the ILM frozen phase to scale the
tier into existence by requiring a minimal amount of memory and storage.

Closes #72771

commit_message:
Improve cleanup of Node Shutdown in tests (#72772)

Makes the following changes:
 - The node shutdown feature flag isn't set on the test runner, only the
   cluster JVMs, so we can't use it to check here. Instead, the cleanup
   now infers whether it's enabled from the shape of the first
   GET `_nodes/shutdown` response.
 - Now uses `adminClient()` instead of `client()`
 - Removes the unnecessary `instanceof` check, which was *not* due to parsing,
   but the fact that `nodes` is indeed a map if the feature flag isn't enabled.

issue_title: [Docs] Limitations of top metrics agg are not fully documented
issue_body:
We have [tested the top_metrics agg](https://github.com/elastic/kibana/issues/98733) as a replacement for top_hits in Lens, and found that [the docs](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/master/search-aggregations-metrics-top-metrics.html) are not as accurate as we hoped. Here are the differences from the docs:

* Top_metrics does not work with multi-value arrays, might be related to https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/53194
* Top_metrics _does_ work with booleans
* Top_metrics does not support scripting
commit_message:
[DOCS] Clarify supported fields for `top_metrics` agg (#73907)

Changes:
* Notes `metrics.field` supports `boolean` fields and runtime fields.
* Notes `metrics.field` doesn't support array values.

Closes #72889

commit_message:
[ML] Revert model snapshot now waits for annotations index (#72926)

Reverting a model snapshot with the delete_intervening_results
option deletes system-generated annotations that are more recent
than the model snapshot. Doing this relies on the annotations
index being available, so the revert model snapshot action now
waits for this.

This problem is more likely to be seen in recent releases, as we
now revert to the most recent model snapshot when a job relocates
from one node to another, so we are more likely to be reverting
a model snapshot at a time when there has been cluster disruption
and this could also be causing the annotations index to be
temporarily unavailable.

Fixes #72917
